title: GMF South and Wider Europe
gist: GMF's South and Wider Europe program landing page; its publication headlines reveal the think tank's active theses on Europe-China exposure, Mediterranean/Gulf energy dependency, and Russia's receding influence across the Black Sea neighborhood.

insights.claim: GMF analysts frame a coming 'China Shock 2.0' as a live risk Europe must brace for, echoing the early-2000s import surge but implying a fresh wave of Chinese overcapacity hitting European industry.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: Watch European trade-defense and tariff moves against Chinese overcapacity as a policy catalyst for exposed EU industrials.

insights.claim: GMF characterizes the Eastern Mediterranean as caught in an energy 'dependency trap,' signaling skepticism that the region's gas resources translate into genuine strategic autonomy.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: Temper expectations for East Med gas as a European supply substitute; dependency dynamics may cap its investment case.

insights.claim: GMF openly questions whether the Gulf's boom is ending, headlining 'The End of the Gulf's Gilded Age?' and implying the region's oil-financed expansion may be losing durability.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: A GMF flag on Gulf economic momentum peaking is a cue to reassess GCC growth and sovereign-fund deployment assumptions.
insights.quote: The End of the Gulf’s Gilded Age?
insights.locator: The End of the Gulf’s Gilded Age?

insights.claim: GMF is examining whether Africa can fill Europe's energy diversification gap, treating African supply as an unproven but strategically important substitute as Europe reduces Russian dependence.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: Track African gas/energy infrastructure deals as Europe's diversification-away-from-Russia trade extends into new suppliers.
insights.quote: Can Africa Fill Europe’s Energy Diversification Gap?
insights.locator: Can Africa Fill Europe’s Energy Diversification Gap?

insights.claim: GMF frames EU-Turkiye ties as an 'interdependence dilemma' spanning trade, energy and security, arguing deep mutual reliance is itself a strategic constraint rather than a stabilizer.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: Monitor EU-Turkiye trade and energy negotiations as a source of tail risk for firms exposed to the customs-union relationship.

themes.theme: Russia's receding influence in the Black Sea neighborhood

insights.claim: GMF assesses that Armenia's June 2026 election chose continuity over ties to Russia, reading the vote as another step in Yerevan's westward drift away from Moscow's orbit.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: A durable Armenia tilt west reshapes South Caucasus connectivity and sanctions-evasion routes worth tracking.
insights.quote: Armenia Chose Continuity Over Ties to Russia
insights.locator: Armenia Chose Continuity Over Ties to Russia

insights.claim: GMF's stated view is that 'Russia Has Lost Moldova,' arguing Moscow's leverage over Chisinau has structurally eroded amid Moldova's EU-accession trajectory.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: A confirmed Moldova pivot supports the EU-enlargement reconstruction thesis and reduces frozen-conflict escalation odds.

insights.claim: GMF reads Russia's expansion of citizenship (passportisation) in Transnistria as a defensive move made 'under pressure,' implying Moscow is trying to entrench claims precisely where its position is weakening.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: Passportisation is a leading indicator of hybrid escalation; watch Transnistria as a flashpoint for Moldova-EU tensions.
insights.quote: Passportisation Under Pressure: Why Russia is Expanding Citizenship in Transnistria

themes.theme: Infrastructure and connectivity as strategic fronts

insights.claim: GMF identifies critical infrastructure as a distinct new 'front' in the Balkans, signaling that ports, grids and telecoms are becoming contested terrain in great-power competition there.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: Infrastructure-security screening in the Balkans is a policy tailwind for European-financed connectivity projects and a headwind for Chinese/Russian bidders.

insights.claim: GMF is tracking the emergence of a 'New Connectivity Hub' and the Middle Corridor across the Black Sea and Caucasus, reflecting a policy bet on trans-Caspian trade routes that bypass Russia.
insights.speaker: German Marshall Fund
insights.so_what: The Middle Corridor build-out is an infrastructure investment theme tied to Europe's de-risking from Russian transit.
insights.quote: The Middle Corridor in the Spotlight
insights.locator: The Middle Corridor in the Spotlight
